summary: Google Calendar is a time management and scheduling service that allows users to create and manage events

steps.title: Create a Google Cloud Project
steps.description: Go to console.cloud.google.com and create a new project
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the Google Cloud Console dashboard with your newly created project
steps.expected_result: You have created a new Google Cloud project

steps.title: Enable Google Calendar API
steps.description: Search for Google Calendar API in the marketplace and click on the result, then click on the Enable button
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the Google Calendar API page with the Enable button
steps.expected_result: Google Calendar API is enabled for your project

steps.title: Create Credentials for Your Project
steps.description: Go to the Navigation menu and click on APIs & Services > Credentials, then click on Create Credentials > OAuth client ID
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the Create credentials page with the OAuth client ID option
steps.expected_result: You have created credentials for your project

steps.title: Create a Service Account and Generate a Private Key
steps.description: Go to the Navigation menu and click on IAM & Admin > Service accounts, then click on Create Service Account and follow the prompts to generate a private key
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the Service accounts page with the Create Service Account button
steps.expected_result: You have created a service account and generated a private key

steps.title: Create a Google Calendar Token
steps.description: Use the private key to authenticate with the Google Calendar API and obtain a token
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the authentication prompt to obtain a token
steps.expected_result: You have obtained a Google Calendar token

common_errors.fix: Your API token is invalid or has been revoked. Generate a new one from the Google Cloud Console
Your API token does not have the required permissions. Set it to have the correct permissions in the Google Cloud Console

pricing_info: Google Calendar API is free for most use cases, but may incur costs for large-scale or commercial use. See the Google Cloud Pricing page for more information
